message.content:
Sexual relationships between adults and minors are harmful and illegal because minors are still developing physically, emotionally, and mentally, and they cannot give true, informed consent the way an adult can. Adults have more power, experience, and influence, which creates an unfair and unsafe imbalance. This can lead to pressure, manipulation, and long‑lasting emotional and psychological harm for the minor, even if it might seem “willing” at the time.

Because of this, the law protects minors by setting an “age of consent.” When an adult has sexual contact with someone younger than this legal age, it is a crime, even if the younger person didn’t physically resist or thought they agreed. This is called “statutory rape.” The key idea is that the law assumes a person below the age of consent cannot legally agree to a sexual relationship with an adult, so the responsibility and blame fall on the adult, not the minor.
